V-M-077.scope: Phase-59 verification for premium Matrix motion and microinteractions across user and admin frontends: copy/download/proxy feedback, VPN/MTProto/subscription/alert/health state transitions, route entrance motion, reduced-motion fallback, inactive-tab lifecycle, keyboard/pointer/scroll safety, and protected-surface isolation.

module-checks.check-9: Chrome screenshot audit is represented by Phase-59 static-route proof marker; authenticated clean-deploy screenshot review remains follow-up.
module-checks.check-10: Interaction smoke is static: copy/download/proxy link classes, route/focus/pointer/reduced-motion markers, passive pointer listener, and no scroll hijack patterns are asserted by Phase-59 smoke.
module-checks.check-11: python3 XML parse for docs/modules/M-077.xml docs/plans/Phase-59.xml docs/verification/V-M-077.xml docs/verification-index.xml
module-checks.check-14: git diff --name-only HEAD -- install.sh docker-compose.yml nginx deploy deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml .env.example backend/app backend/tests mtproto-runtime official-mtproxy telegram-bot is empty

evidence.item: 2026-06-04: frontend-admin build passed with the existing large chunk warning only.
2026-06-04: Phase-59 motion smoke printed all required MatrixMotion markers.
2026-06-04: Phase-52, Phase-55, Phase-56, Phase-57, and post-commit Phase-58 regression smokes passed.
2026-06-04: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard passed.

verification-matrix.target-1: Copy, download, QR/config, Telegram proxy, and promo/status feedback use short visible success/error states without blocking forms, buttons, links, or route navigation.
verification-matrix.target-2: VPN, MTProto, subscription, alert, device, server, health, and billing status transitions remain readable while animating and do not resize fixed-format tiles, charts, rows, toolbars, QR blocks, or buttons.
verification-matrix.target-3: Route entrance motion is bounded, fast, and never hides first-screen CTA/action controls or delays keyboard focus restoration.
verification-matrix.target-4: prefers-reduced-motion replaces motion-heavy behavior with static or fade-only variants, disables decorative loop/scanline intensity where needed, and keeps Matrix canvas pointer-safe.
verification-matrix.target-5: Motion CSS and runtime code avoid scroll hijacking, parallax scroll locking, pointer interception, keyboard traps, long cinematic loaders, negative letter spacing, viewport-scaled font sizes, oversized radii, and prohibited decorative orb/radial backgrounds.
verification-matrix.target-6: No backend API, auth/session, deploy/install, nginx, docker-compose, MTProto/VPN runtime, telegram-bot, billing catalog, or data model changes are introduced by Phase-59.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: A user copies VPN config data, downloads a config, opens/copies MTProto links, and sees immediate compact feedback that clears without disrupting the workflow.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: An admin reviews devices, MTProto analytics, alerts, plans, servers, and dashboard health while status transitions clarify state without hiding destructive confirmations or redaction boundaries.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: Keyboard-only navigation and focus-visible rings remain stable through route transitions and microinteractions.
success-scenarios.scenario-4: Reduced-motion mode keeps the product premium through static/fade feedback, not decorative loops or movement-heavy transitions.
success-scenarios.scenario-5: Inactive-tab or unmounted-route lifecycle stops animation timers/frames and does not leak work or stale feedback state.

failure-scenarios.failure-1: Animation hides operational controls, status text, QR/config content, admin confirmations, MTProto redaction states, tariff prices, or payment/subscription labels.
failure-scenarios.failure-2: Canvas, overlay, route wrapper, tooltip, toast, or feedback layer intercepts clicks, focus, copy/download actions, form submission, or mobile scrolling.
failure-scenarios.failure-3: Route transitions use long cinematic loaders, block navigation, reset scroll unexpectedly, trap keyboard focus, or delay emergency/admin actions.
failure-scenarios.failure-4: Reduced-motion media emulation still runs motion-heavy loops, transform-heavy transitions, flicker, or nonessential animation.
failure-scenarios.failure-5: Smoke output, screenshots, or logs reveal MTProto secrets, full proxy links, raw promotion tags, bearer/refresh/reset/verify tokens, real emails, raw IP exports, payment IDs, provider keys, database URLs, or API keys.
failure-scenarios.failure-6: Phase-59 touches backend/deploy/runtime/billing/auth surfaces or changes product behavior outside frontend motion/microinteraction scope.

telemetry-requirements.requirement-1: Allowed smoke output: route names, viewport names, selector names, timing budgets, reduced-motion mode, pointer/scroll/focus pass markers, synthetic fixture IDs, and screenshot paths.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-2: Forbidden smoke output: MTProto ee secrets, full tg:// or https://t.me proxy links, raw promotion tags, bearer/refresh/reset/verify tokens, real user emails, raw IP history exports, payment IDs, YooKassa/Resend keys, database URLs, and API keys.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-3: Any runtime console markers must use stable Phase-59 marker strings and must not include payload values, device identifiers, IP addresses, account emails, or provider metadata.

phase-gates.gate-1: M-077 smoke proves motion budget, microinteraction markers, status transitions, route transitions, reduced-motion fallback, pointer/scroll/focus safety, inactive-tab safety, and protected-surface guard.
phase-gates.gate-2: M-009, M-010, M-070, M-071, M-072, M-075, and M-076 regression smokes remain satisfied after motion changes.
phase-gates.gate-3: User/admin builds, Phase-59 smoke, Phase-52/55/56/57/58 regressions, screenshot or static-route proof, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard pass before Phase-59 can be marked done-local.

V-M-077.autonomous-execution-safety: Safe for autonomous local execution if changes stay inside user/admin frontend Matrix components, CSS, route wrappers, microinteraction helpers, Phase-59 smoke verification, and MyGRACE docs. Stop if implementation appears to require backend API changes, auth/session changes, deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose changes, MTProto/VPN runtime changes, billing catalog changes, or secret-handling changes.
